name: bluetooth

documentation.readme: Go Bluetooth is a cross-platform package for using Bluetooth Low Energy hardware from the Go programming language.
usage_sections: Installation: You need to have a fairly recent version of BlueZ, for example v5.48 is the latest released version for Ubuntu/Debian. sudo apt update sudo apt install bluez Once you have done this, you can obtain the Go Bluetooth package using Git: git clone https://github.com/tinygo-org/blueto

Installation: In order to compile Go Bluetooth code targeting macOS, you must do so on macOS itself. In other words, we do not currently have cross compiler support. You must also have XCode tools installed: xcode-select --install Once you have done this, you can obtain the Go Bluetooth packag

Installation: Only the Go compiler itself is needed to compile Go Bluetooth code targeting Windows. You can obtain the Go Bluetooth package using Git: git clone https://github.com/tinygo-org/bluetooth.git

Installation: You must install TinyGo to be able to compile bare metal code using Go Bluetooth. Follow the instructions for your operating system at https://tinygo.org/getting-started/ Once you have installed TinyGo, you can install the Go Bluetooth package by running: git clone https://github

arguments.description: Increase log verbosity (repeatable for higher levels).

arguments.description: Extra HTTP header in key:value form (repeatable).

arguments.description: Skip TLS certificate verification (lab use only).

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with paypal/gatt (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with graipher/bluewalker (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with go-ble/ble (score=10); pair in the chain planner.

Phase 3: same attack_surface=['ble_4_x', 'ble_5_x', 'ble_mesh'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

Phase 4: same attack_surface=['ble_4_x', 'ble_5_x', 'ble_mesh'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

Phase 5: same attack_surface=['ble_4_x', 'ble_5_x', 'ble_mesh'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [ble_4_x, ble_5_x, ble_mesh] in the Bluetooth category. Fits when the engagement needs: [![Go Bluetooth](./images/gobluetooth.png)](https://tinygo.org/bluetooth).
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: Go Bluetooth is a cross-platform package for using Bluetooth Low Energy hardware from the Go programming language.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: When you need Bluetooth, use bluetooth because [![Go Bluetooth](./images/gobluetooth.png)](https://tinygo.org/bluetooth).
When chaining Bluetooth tools, pair bluetooth with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `bluetooth --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
